file1.content: {@}module_file_name: config_manager_base.py`n{@}module_description: This module provides an advanced, robust, and flexible configuration management system with support for JSON, YAML, environment variables, encryption, and hot-reloading.`n{@}module_purpose: Centralize and manage complex configuration settings from multiple sources with enhanced security and dynamic updating capabilities.`n{@}module_intent: Implement a thread-safe, asynchronous configuration manager with hierarchical support, encryption for sensitive data, hot-reloading, and schema validation.`n{@}module_dir_path_rel: python/config_mgmt_services/config_manager_base.py`n{@}module_dir_path_abs: C:\\Users\\pinea\\UTILITIES\\SERVICES\\T4AI\\MicroServices\\tazeeka-fusion\\python\\config_mgmt_services\\config_manager_base.py`n{@}module_constraints: Python 3.9+, thread-safety considerations, proper encryption key management, external libraries: see dependencies`n{@}module_dependencies: <# TODO: Please populate-values-here-for-all-External-Libraries-Used-in-Codebase. In addition, including, for example: Go Micro (for microservices), Rust (for performance-critical components), Docker (for plugin sandboxing)`n{@}version: 0.0.1`n{@}last_modified_date: 2024-11-01`n{@}last_modified_time: 08:08:08`n{@}last_modified_timezone: PST`n{@}published_by: Tools-4-AI.io (DigiCore Services LLC)`n{@}author: Daniel Claussen (DanozWorld)

file2.content: {#} FileName: "color_scheme_base.py"`n{#} FileVersion: "0.0.1"`n{#} FileDirPathRel: "services/claude-engineer/src/ui/color_scheme_base.py"`n{#} FileDirPathFullAbs: "%USERPROFILE%\UTILITIES\\utils_srvcs_t4ai_mai_toolz\\global_platform\\services\\claude-engineer\\src\\ui\\color_scheme_base.py"  {#} noqa: E501`n{#} Publisher: DigiCore Services LLC`n{#} Author: Danny`n{#} DateTimeCreated: 2024-07-15_03-03-03am_PST`n{#} DateTimeLastModified: 2024-07-15_03-03-03am_PST`n{#} DescriptionBrief: "Base class for color scheme management"`n{#} DescriptionDetailed: "Provides base functionality for console color scheme management"  {#} noqa: E501`n{#} Dependencies: "colorama, pygments, typing"`n{#} Constraints: "Python 3.9+"`n

file1.content: Please generate in markdown format (in dir path: '{project_dir_root}/docs/deliverables/' and write for me detailed, advanced, comprehensive, complex, and fully fleshed-out detailed Functional requirements, RTM, Use cases, Technical Detailed Specification, Testing Plan, and more - each referencing each other. For all docs, include a TOC, with all content visually appealing using the Dillinger, Dragon Ball, and other best practices' for markdown language format:<//>Add a TOC (Table of Contents) at the beginning of all docs, with actionable 'jump' or 'hper-links' to each corresponding section; at a minimum, for the FRS (and similar for others as appropriate and relevant), include the following sections (add more sections based on what you know of my current requirements, specifications, code and logic, goals, objectives and what I'm aiming to achieve with my goals, objectives, and stretch-goals for a feature-rich, robust, modular, reliable, extensible app uxing Hexagonal and clean architecture with DDD/EDA-hybrid design patterns, Microservices-first): Summary, Purpose/Intent, Audience, User Roles, User Personas, detailed Functional Requirements, Non-Functional Requirements, real-life Use Cases, Process Flow sequences and other diagrams in mermaid format using """mermaid {diagram/flow content} """ - use " instead of backtick due to platform limitations, and I'll replace afterwards; include many various process, sequence flow, and other diagrams (C4, Component, Use Cases, etc., including architectural, design, and business process flows, etc.), Technical requirements, Design and Architecture requirements, Design Decisions and Alternatives, Project Plan, Test Cases and Plan (including Automated Testing, User Testing, Performance Testing, Load Testing, negative testing, - also, include 'edge' cases, etc.), External Resources and References (including URLs and links to sites where the reader can review additional resources offline), Glossary, Definitions, Future Enhancements and Improvements, Appendix, etc. For all content and each section, wherever possible, please include a) the hierarchical structure of Category, Sub-Category, Type, Sub-Type, etc., and b) a listing of 'Pros/Cons', 'SWOT and Comparison Analysis', Constraints, Dependencies, Future Improvements and Enhancements, Futuristic Considerations (to eleveat to 'science fiction' realm of ideation), Alternatives, etc. - for readability and improved organization and structure, etc.  ** NOTE: For both the 'Use Cases' and 'Test Cases' sections, please add as many diverse and unique common and 'edge' real-life Use Cases (including for multiple disciplines, industries, etc. - e.g., Scientific community, Education community, Business community, Sales and Marketing, Communications, Branding, R&D - Research and Development, IT Tech, Software and Application Engineering, Architectural and Emgineering (physical buildings, homes, etc.), Fashion Design, Interior Design, and a very important crucial aspect that includes "Customer Relationship Management" and "Customer Support", including AI-assisted 'ChatBots' and other capabilities, etc.), and Test Cases (to cover 100% of the Requirements, Non-Functional requirements, and Use Cases), as appropriate. Ensure you include an RTM (Requirements Traceability Matrix), in a table format, to 'trace' all use cases, requirements, testing, etc. to the design and requirement sections. Ensure you uniquely number and assign a UID (unique ID) to all sections within all documents including Requirements, TDD, Solution Blueprint, RTM, Use Case, etc. so this 'tracing' can be done properly in the Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM). Also, for ALL docs, please organize the structure in a hierarchical manner, with sections including, at a minimum, by 'Category', 'Sub-Category', 'Type', and 'Sub-Type' (you populate based on the content and my key goals & objectives for this request). Add others that you feel would be beneficial and informative to me and future readers.  **VERY IMPORTANT** Please ensure that in Req's, TDD, and others where appropriate you reference comprehensive Configuration Management System, where all levels include an 'enabled' flag (nested structure, with arrays, etc.) and where appropriate in my config files (especially JSON config). The JSON Config should also include '$<placeholder-values>' for all static and sensitive information, which will be substituted and replaced using methods in my codebase. and abbreviations used throughout the document(s) and their definitions.  ** NOTE: Please also include a 'References' section that includes a list of all references, citations, and sources used
